_meta.description: Deterministic rule set for student visa eligibility. NOT legal advice. Reflects publicly documented requirements as of the MVP snapshot; intended to be refreshed via the policy ingestion layer.
changelog: 0.3.0: Rules enriched with priority (CRITICAL|IMPORTANT|OPTIONAL), why_it_matters, what_to_do, and estimated_time so the engine can emit actionable next steps.
0.2.0: Offer letter and proof-of-funds rules switched to evidence-status checks.

rules.why_it_matters: Without a valid passport the visa application cannot be submitted or the visa sticker affixed.
rules.what_to_do: Renew your passport so it covers the full duration of your intended studies plus a buffer.

rules.description: A Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) from a licensed sponsor is required.
rules.why_it_matters: The CAS is the single most important piece of evidence: the UKVI will not issue a Student visa without it.
rules.what_to_do: Secure an unconditional offer from a UKVI-licensed sponsor, then request your CAS reference.

rules.description: Proof of funds covering course fees and living costs.
evidence_required: Bank statements showing required maintenance funds
rules.why_it_matters: UKVI maintenance rules require funds to be held for 28 consecutive days before application.
rules.what_to_do: Prepare bank statements showing tuition + UKVI maintenance amounts held for 28+ days, or arrange an official sponsorship letter.

rules.description: Secure English Language Test (SELT) evidence, typically IELTS UKVI with minimum CEFR B2 for degree-level study.
rules.why_it_matters: A valid SELT at the correct CEFR level is mandatory for most Student Route applicants.
rules.what_to_do: Book an approved SELT (e.g. IELTS UKVI) at a UKVI-approved centre and achieve the required overall/component scores.

rules.description: Prior education should align with the intended course level.
rules.why_it_matters: Sponsors assess your academic record to decide whether you meet entry requirements.
rules.what_to_do: Gather official transcripts and certificates; obtain certified translations if not in English.

rules.description: Valid passport covering the intended study period.
rules.why_it_matters: IRCC will not issue a study permit beyond your passport's validity.
rules.what_to_do: Renew your passport to cover the full program length plus buffer.

rules.description: Letter of Acceptance from a Designated Learning Institution (DLI).
rules.why_it_matters: A Letter of Acceptance from a DLI is mandatory for the study permit application.
rules.what_to_do: Secure admission to a DLI listed on the IRCC DLI list and obtain the official LOA.

rules.description: Proof of sufficient funds for tuition and living costs.
rules.why_it_matters: IRCC requires demonstrable funds for tuition + first-year living costs for you and any accompanying dependents.
rules.what_to_do: Purchase a Guaranteed Investment Certificate (GIC) from a participating bank or prepare 4–6 months of bank statements.

rules.description: Language proficiency is often required by institutions (IELTS 6.0 typical minimum).
rules.why_it_matters: Most Canadian institutions require an English (or French) proficiency test for admission; it strengthens your study permit file.
rules.what_to_do: Book an IELTS (Academic) or TOEFL iBT and meet the target institution's minimum.

rules.description: Academic record appropriate for the chosen program.
rules.why_it_matters: Institutions and IRCC both look at prior education to assess fit and credibility.
rules.what_to_do: Collect official transcripts and diplomas; get notarised translations where required.

rules.why_it_matters: German missions require a valid passport with sufficient remaining validity and blank pages.
rules.what_to_do: Renew or extend your passport so it is valid for the full initial visa period.

rules.description: Admission letter (Zulassungsbescheid) from a recognised German HEI, or proof of application.
rules.why_it_matters: The Zulassungsbescheid is the central evidence for a student visa application; without it only a limited applicant visa is possible.
rules.what_to_do: Apply via uni-assist or direct to the HEI and secure the admission letter.

rules.description: Proof of financial resources, typically via a blocked account (Sperrkonto).
evidence_required: Blocked account confirmation / scholarship / sponsorship letter
rules.why_it_matters: German missions require documented financial resources (Finanzierungsnachweis) before issuing the visa.
rules.what_to_do: Open a blocked account with a recognised provider and deposit the annual minimum, or provide a formal scholarship/sponsorship.

rules.description: Language proficiency in German or English, depending on program.
evidence_required: IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught programs, or TestDaF/DSH for German-taught programs
rules.why_it_matters: Language proficiency is an admission requirement for the HEI and supports the visa case.
rules.what_to_do: Sit IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught programs, or TestDaF/DSH for German-taught programs.

rules.why_it_matters: Your prior qualifications must be recognised as a Hochschulzugangsberechtigung for the chosen program.
rules.what_to_do: Check recognition on anabin.kmk.org and gather certified copies of your certificates and transcripts.
